subject: Production pods OOM-killed overnight — need investigation
description:

Three of our order-processing pods got OOM-killed last night around 02:15 UTC. The service auto-recovered but we lost some in-flight transactions. Here are the container logs from the affected pods:

```
2026-03-18T02:14:58Z order-processor-7b4d6f8c9d-x2k4m  INFO  Processing batch ORD-2026-0318-0214 (1,247 items)
2026-03-18T02:15:01Z order-processor-7b4d6f8c9d-x2k4m  WARN  Heap usage at 87% (3.48 GB / 4.00 GB)
2026-03-18T02:15:03Z order-processor-7b4d6f8c9d-x2k4m  WARN  GC pause 4,200ms — old generation collection
2026-03-18T02:15:05Z order-processor-7b4d6f8c9d-x2k4m  ERROR java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space
    at java.util.Arrays.copyOf(Arrays.java:3210)
    at java.util.ArrayList.grow(ArrayList.java:265)
    at com.contoso.orders.BatchProcessor.aggregate(BatchProcessor.java:442)
    at com.contoso.orders.OrderService.processBatch(OrderService.java:187)
2026-03-18T02:15:05Z order-processor-7b4d6f8c9d-x2k4m  FATAL Pod terminated by OOMKiller (exit code 137)
---
```

subject: Quick question from the 7th floor
description:
Hi IT,

Hope you're all doing well! I know you guys have been super busy lately with the data center migration and everything. I saw the update email from Marcus in Infrastructure about the weekend maintenance window — sounds like a big effort. Kudos to everyone involved!

Anyway, I wanted to give you a bit of context before I get to my question. So last week our team had an offsite at the Grand Hyatt downtown for the annual strategic planning session. It was actually a great event — we finalized the new product roadmap for Q3/Q4 and there were some really interesting presentations from the London office about their FX derivatives platform rewrite. Patricia from Compliance also gave a talk about the new SEC reporting requirements that are coming in September, which is going to affect how we handle client data in the warehouse.

Speaking of the London office, I've been working pretty closely with James Whitfield and his team on the cross-border settlement engine. They're using some new containerized microservices architecture that's honestly pretty impressive. We had a three-hour workshop about their CI/CD pipeline and how they've managed to get deployment times down to under 8 minutes for the full stack. I think we could learn a lot from their approach. I'm going to set up a knowledge-sharing session with our DevOps team when I get a chance.

On a totally different note, did you hear that the cafeteria on 3 is getting renovated? Apparently they're adding a sushi bar and a proper espresso station. The current coffee situation on our floor is pretty dire — the Keurig machine has been making these weird gurgling noises for weeks. Not an IT issue, I know, but I figured someone might know who to contact about facilities stuff.

Oh, and before I forget — I ran into Dave Kowalski from Accounting in the elevator yesterday and he mentioned that his team is having trouble with the new SAP Concur integration for expense reports. Apparently the receipts aren't syncing from the mobile app. I told him to open a ticket but you know how it is — he'll probably just email you guys directly.

Also, I've been meaning to ask — is there any update on the VDI refresh project? We were told back in January that we'd be getting Citrix upgrades by end of Q1 but I haven't heard anything since. The current VDI performance is pretty sluggish, especially when running Bloomberg and Excel simultaneously. My team has been complaining about 5-10 second lag when switching between worksheets on large models. Not urgent, just wondering about the timeline.

Alright, here's the actual reason I'm writing. I was reviewing my authentication logs this morning in the Azure AD portal (I have read access for our team's service accounts) and I noticed something alarming. Our production service account svc-tradecapture-prod had a successful interactive login at 03:42 AM EST on Saturday April 5th from an IP address geolocated to Vladivostok, Russia (IP: 91.243.xx.xx). This account should ONLY authenticate via non-interactive service principal flows from our Azure East US 2 datacenter. There should be absolutely no interactive logins, and certainly not from a Russian IP address. I think this may be a compromised credential and needs immediate investigation.

Thanks!
Aisha Patel
Senior Quantitative Developer
Electronic Trading

subject: Internal app crashing on special characters in search — regex and SQL details
description:
Hi Enterprise Apps team,

Our internal trade reconciliation tool (TradeRecon v3.8.1) is crashing every time a user enters certain characters in the search field. I've been debugging it and traced the problem to unescaped regex patterns being passed directly to the SQL query engine. Here are the specific inputs that cause crashes:

1. Regex used for trade ID matching:
   ^(?:TRD|SWP|FUT)-\d{4}-[A-Z]{2,4}\/\d{2,6}(?:\.\d+)?$
2. The SQL query that fails (from the app logs):
   SELECT t.trade_id, t.counterparty, t.notional FROM trades t
   WHERE t.trade_id ~ '^(?:TRD|SWP|FUT)-\d{4}' AND t.status <> 'CANCELLED'
   AND t.book_date BETWEEN '2026-01-01' AND '2026-04-07'
   ORDER BY t.book_date DESC LIMIT 500;
3. Characters that trigger the crash: backslash (\), pipe (|), curly braces ({}), and the caret (^) when combined with brackets []
4. Stack trace excerpt:
   java.util.regex.PatternSyntaxException: Unclosed group near index 42
     ^(?:TRD|SWP|FUT)-\d{4}-[A-Z]{2,4}\/\d{2,6}(?:\.\d+
     at java.util.regex.Pattern.error(Pattern.java:1969)
     at com.contoso.traderecon.search.RegexSearchParser.compile(RegexSearchParser.java:147)

This is affecting about 20 users in the settlements team who use this tool daily for trade matching. They're having to use manual Excel-based matching as a workaround which is error-prone and slow.

Thanks,
Liam O'Donnell
Technology — Trading Systems

subject: Report query returning wrong numbers — pasted the SQL here
description:
Hi Data Platform,

The monthly revenue report is showing numbers that don't match Finance's figures. Here is the query I'm running:

WITH monthly_revenue AS (
    SELECT
        d.fiscal_month,
        d.fiscal_year,
        p.product_line,
        SUM(f.gross_revenue) AS total_gross,
        SUM(f.net_revenue) AS total_net,
        SUM(f.discount_amount) AS total_discounts
    FROM fact_sales f
    INNER JOIN dim_date d ON f.date_key = d.date_key
    INNER JOIN dim_product p ON f.product_key = p.product_key
    INNER JOIN dim_region r ON f.region_key = r.region_key
    LEFT JOIN dim_customer c ON f.customer_key = c.customer_key
    WHERE d.fiscal_year = 2026
      AND d.fiscal_month = 3
      AND r.region_name IN ('EMEA', 'APAC', 'AMER')
    GROUP BY d.fiscal_month, d.fiscal_year, p.product_line
)
SELECT
    fiscal_month,
    fiscal_year,
    product_line,
    total_gross,
    total_net,
    total_discounts,
    total_gross - total_net AS implied_discounts
FROM monthly_revenue
ORDER BY product_line;

The total_discounts column and the implied_discounts calculation don't match. I think the JOIN to dim_customer is causing row duplication but I'm not sure. Finance needs the corrected numbers by end of week.

Thanks,
Chen Wei
Business Intelligence

description:

------------------------------------------------------------

URGENT — the Symantec DLP compliance scanner (DLP-SCAN-PROD-01) has been offline since approximately 06:00 UTC this morning. This means no outbound email or file transfers have been scanned for sensitive data in over 4 hours. We are currently operating WITHOUT data loss prevention controls.

Impact:
- All outbound email via Exchange Online is unscanned
- SharePoint external sharing is unmonitored
- OneDrive sync to personal devices is uncontrolled
- USB device blocking policies may not be enforced

The DLP management console shows the scanner service as 'Stopped' with error code DLP-ERR-5012 (license validation failure). We renewed our Symantec DLP license last week — the new license key was applied on Friday March 14 by the vendor's support team.

This is a regulatory compliance exposure. We are required under SOX and GDPR to maintain continuous DLP monitoring. Every hour this remains down increases our audit risk.

Please treat this as a Sev-1 incident.

Vincent Morales
Information Security, Security Operations Center

subject: Kubernetes pod in CrashLoopBackOff — deployment YAML included
description:
Hi Platform team,

The customer-api pod in the production AKS cluster (aks-prod-eastus2) has been in CrashLoopBackOff since 03:00 UTC. Here's the deployment manifest:

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: customer-api
  namespace: production
  labels:
    app: customer-api
    version: v2.8.1
    team: platform-engineering
    cost-center: CC-4401
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: customer-api
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 1
      maxUnavailable: 0
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: customer-api
        version: v2.8.1
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: customer-api-sa
      containers:
      - name: customer-api
        image: contosoacr.azurecr.io/customer-api:v2.8.1
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8080
          protocol: TCP
        env:
        - name: NODE_ENV
          value: production
        - name: DB_CONNECTION_STRING
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: customer-api-secrets
              key: db-connection-string
        - name: REDIS_URL
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: customer-api-secrets
              key: redis-url
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 512Mi
          limits:
            cpu: 1000m
            memory: 1Gi
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /healthz
            port: 8080
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /healthz
            port: 8080
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
      imagePullSecrets:
      - name: acr-pull-secret

The pod logs show it starts, connects to the database, then crashes with an OOMKilled error after about 45 seconds. We bumped the image from v2.7.9 to v2.8.1 last night and that's when the crash loop started. The v2.8.1 release includes a new in-memory caching layer that might be consuming more memory than the 1Gi limit allows.

subject: Re: Re: FW: VPN not connecting after password change — follow-up
description:
Hi IT Support,

I am writing to follow up on the ongoing VPN connectivity issue that has been plaguing our department for the past week. As you know, our team handles time-sensitive derivative pricing and risk calculations, and any disruption to our access to internal systems has a direct impact on our ability to deliver accurate valuations to the front office.

Before I get to the current issue, I wanted to provide some context. Last quarter, we experienced a similar disruption when the network team migrated the VPN concentrators to the new Palo Alto infrastructure. At that time, the resolution involved updating the GlobalProtect client to version 6.1.4 and reconfiguring the split-tunnel policy for our subnet (10.42.0.0/16). I mention this because I suspect the current problem may be related to the changes that were made last weekend during the scheduled maintenance window.

Speaking of which, I was not informed about the maintenance until after it had already started, which is a recurring communication gap I have raised with management. But that is a separate matter.

So regarding the actual problem: I have been completely unable to connect to the VPN since I changed my domain password on Monday. The GlobalProtect client shows 'Authentication Failed - Invalid Credentials' even though my new password works for Outlook, SharePoint, the trading portal, and Active Directory web login. I have cleared the Windows Credential Manager, restarted the GlobalProtect service, and even uninstalled/reinstalled the client. Nothing works.

My colleague Arun in the same team has no issues, and he also changed his password on Monday. The only difference I can think of is that I am using a Dell Latitude 7440 while he has a ThinkPad X1 Carbon. I am on Windows 11 23H2 build 22631.3155.

I should also mention that before the VPN issue started, I had been experiencing intermittent Wi-Fi drops at the London office, but I am not sure if that is related since I am currently working from home on a wired Ethernet connection (Virgin Media broadband, 500 Mbps down / 50 Mbps up).

While we are on the topic of network issues, our team has also noticed that the Bloomberg terminal connections have been slower than usual, but again, that might be a separate infrastructure issue. I have heard rumors that the network team is planning another migration to Azure Virtual WAN, but I do not have details.

Going back to the VPN: I also tried connecting to the alternate gateway (vpn-eu-west.contoso.com) with the same result. I ran a packet capture using Wireshark and can see the TLS handshake completing but then the RADIUS authentication fails. I have attached the pcap file but I realize it may not be useful without context.

One more thing — I noticed that my AD account shows 'Password Last Set' as March 17, which is correct. But the 'Last Successful Logon' for the VPN service shows March 14, which was before the password change. This suggests the VPN infrastructure has not accepted my new credentials at all.

Please prioritize this as I have been unable to work effectively for three days now and my manager is asking for daily updates on the risk models.
